touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Nathan Iyer at QuantumLink. Rep introduced AskElephant's forecasting automation capabilities for CS teams. Nathan expressed interest in improving forecast accuracy for their 600+ person org, mentioned current manual processes are time-consuming. Brief discovery call.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently managing forecast accuracy across your CS team?
questions_asked.answer: We're doing it mostly manually in spreadsheets. It's taking our team leads 3-4 hours a week and still feels like guesswork.

next_steps: Send overview deck and case study
Schedule 15-minute follow-up to discuss specific use cases

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with product overview and case study for similar mid-market SaaS company. Nathan responded same day confirming interest and suggesting a deeper discovery call next week.

questions_asked.question: Do you have any customers in our vertical who are using this for CS forecasting specifically?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have three vertical SaaS companies using our CS forecasting module. Happy to share references.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Nathan covering QuantumLink's current forecasting challenges. Team of 27 account managers struggling with pipeline visibility and accuracy. Nathan mentioned they're evaluating Gong as well. Discussed integration requirements with their existing Salesforce and Gainsight stack. Nathan is champion, mentioned need to involve exec sponsor (Grayson Yang, SVP Revenue) and technical team for eval.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: Our board is pushing for better predictability. Last quarter we missed our retention forecast by 12%. It's become a priority.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Grayson Yang is our SVP of Revenue and he'll need to sign off. We'll also need IT and a few of the AMs to test it out.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Gong for our use case?
questions_asked.answer: We're typically 20-30% lower for CS-focused forecasting since we're purpose-built for this, while Gong is broader sales intelligence.

objections: We're also looking at Gong. They have more brand recognition in the market.
next_steps: Send detailed integration requirements doc
Intro to technical team for eval
Schedule exec briefing with Grayson

touches.ai_summary: Sent technical requirements document and integration overview. Nathan confirmed receipt and introduced Nathan Doyle (IT Director) for technical evaluation. Set up Slack channel for technical questions.
next_steps: Nathan Doyle to schedule technical eval session
Share security documentation

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with security docs, SOC2 report, and reference customer contacts. Nathan shared internally with IT and exec team. Confirmed technical eval scheduled for next week.
next_steps: Technical eval session scheduled
Prepare demo environment with QuantumLink's data structure

touches.ai_summary: Deep technical evaluation with Nathan Doyle. Walked through API integrations, data model, security architecture, and deployment options. Nathan tested sandbox environment, ran through common workflows. Discussed SSO setup, data retention policies, and webhook configurations. Nathan flagged questions about scale (27 seats, potential to expand to 40+). Gong comparison came up regarding data processing speed.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data residency requirements? We have some EU customers.
questions_asked.answer: We support regional data storage. Can configure EU data to stay in our Frankfurt region.

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline typically look like?
questions_asked.answer: For your size, 2-3 weeks for full integration and team onboarding. We provide dedicated implementation support.

questions_asked.question: Are there any integration gaps or concerns from your side?
questions_asked.answer: No major gaps. We'd want to test the Gainsight webhook reliability in our staging environment first.

objections: Gong's processing is near real-time. Your 15-minute data sync lag could be an issue for our high-velocity team.
next_steps: Set up staging environment test
Provide performance benchmarking data
Schedule follow-up technical session

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up technical call to address data sync timing concerns. Demonstrated optimized configuration that reduces lag to 5 minutes for high-priority signals. Nathan tested performance in staging environment, confirmed acceptable. Discussed ongoing support model and SLA options.

questions_asked.question: What does your premium support tier include?
questions_asked.answer: Premium includes 1-hour response SLA, dedicated Slack channel, quarterly business reviews, and priority feature requests.

next_steps: Nathan to brief Grayson and Nathan Iyer on technical findings
Prepare exec business case presentation

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Grayson Yang (SVP Revenue) and Nathan Iyer. Originally scheduled for last week, rescheduled due to Grayson's conflict. Presented business case: projected impact on forecast accuracy, ROI model, implementation plan. Grayson focused on competitive positioning vs Gong and pricing. Discussed 27-seat deployment with potential expansion. Grayson cautious about budget, mentioned need to stay within allocated software spend for quarter.

questions_asked.question: What's the cost difference at our scale versus Gong?
questions_asked.answer: For 27 seats with unlimited automation tier, we're coming in around $21K annual. Comparable Gong setup would be $32-35K based on their public pricing.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer for you to move forward this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: We need to stay under $22K all-in for this fiscal year. And we need confidence this will actually move the needle on forecast accuracy.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter. We're already over on software spend and need to be careful.
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options
Provide customer ROI case studies
Nathan to coordinate pilot group of 5 AMs for trial

touches.ai_summary: Sent formal proposal: $29,400 list price for 27 seats unlimited_automation tier, with promotional discount bringing it to $20,900 annually (28.9% discount to fit budget). Included ROI calculator and three customer references. Nathan confirmed sharing with Grayson and finance.
next_steps: Await feedback from exec team
Stand by for negotiation call

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Nathan Iyer. Walked through proposal line-by-line. Nathan pushed back on annual commitment, preferred quarterly given uncertainty. Discussed discount structure (28.9% to hit $20,900 target). Nathan concerned about onboarding timeline and whether team will actually adopt it. Agreed to include 2 additional onboarding sessions and 90-day check-in. Nathan mentioned Gong is still in consideration but AskElephant is leading due to CS-specific functionality and price.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure as annual contract with quarterly payments. Would need to reduce the discount slightly to 25%, bringing it to $22,050.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for decision? Do you have a hard deadline?
questions_asked.answer: We need to decide by end of month to get this in for Q2 kickoff. Grayson wants a decision next week.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to expand to more seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at prorated cost. We typically see teams expand after first quarter when they see results.

objections: Annual upfront is tough for our cash flow right now. We'd need quarterly or at least semi-annual payment terms.
next_steps: Send revised proposal with quarterly payment option
Schedule call with finance to discuss payment terms
Coordinate final decision call with Grayson

touches.ai_summary: Sent revised proposal: annual contract at $20,900 with quarterly payments (kept full discount to stay competitive). Included enhanced onboarding package (4 sessions instead of 2) and 60-day opt-out clause to address adoption risk. Nathan confirmed passing to Grayson and legal for review.
next_steps: Legal review expected to take 3-5 days
Stand by for final pricing call with decision makers

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call with Nathan Iyer. Confirmed terms: $20,900 annual with quarterly payments, 27 seats, unlimited_automation tier, enhanced onboarding, 60-day opt-out. Nathan confirmed Grayson approved budget and legal cleared contract structure. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline (target: 2 weeks). Nathan mentioned Gong is out of consideration due to price and broader (less CS-focused) feature set. Ready to move to contracting.

questions_asked.question: Are we clear to send over the final contract?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, send it over. Grayson gave the green light this morning. We're ready to move forward.

next_steps: Send final contract via DocuSign
Schedule implementation kickoff
Coordinate with IT for environment setup
